subject: Joni Ernst in South Georgia: We Must Defend the Majority for Our Children and Grandchildren
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/f68f0c946efc/watch-senator-david-perdue-on-fox-news-georgia-is-the-last-line-of-defense-66411?e=02cd889ad3)
---
|
---
| _**Joni Ernst in South Georgia: We Must Defend the Majority for Our Children and Grandchildren **_ Folks -

We had another great day on the road with Iowa Senator Joni Ernst with stops in Crisp, Thomas, and Lowndes counties!
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| **JONI ERNST→ **
_“David and I worked on the 2018 farm bill together, making sure that Georgia farmers have a fighting chance out there to feed the world... As a combat veteran, and having served in different installations here in the great state of Georgia, how important it is that we are supporting our military men and women, those that are defending our nation. David and I have been serving on the Armed Services Committee together, and I can tell you that every year we’re working through the National Defense Authorization Act, making sure that we are providing the resources necessary for the men and women serving in uniform.”_

**DAVID PERDUE → **
_“We’re on the second day of our second 2020 bus tour around Georgia. This is the ‘Win Georgia, Save America’ tour... You got to understand what [Democrats] want to do: This is the dangerous part. This is the part Kelly and I - we didn’t know this was going to happen - but it’s our task to hold these two seats with your help.” _

**FOX NEWS LIVE FROM MACON -- >**
Yesterday morning, Senator Perdue [joined](https://perduesenate.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ebb829fcdaef555ddec2107e&id=50b3e964cc&e=02cd889ad3) _Fox & Friends_ ahead of a full day on the trail.
---
| [ ](https://perduesenate.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ebb829fcdaef555ddec2107e&id=77754914eb&e=02cd889ad3 "Sen. David Perdue Discusses Senate Runoff on Fox & Friends")
---
| **WFXL-TV:[ Senator Perdue makes stop in Cordele as part of campaign tour](https://perduesenate.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ebb829fcdaef555ddec2107e&id=3d8a0a4c49&e=02cd889ad3)

WALB-TV: [David Perdue makes campaign stop in Crisp Co.](https://perduesenate.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ebb829fcdaef555ddec2107e&id=77014b5b82&e=02cd889ad3)

WGXA-TV: [Senators Perdue, Ernst Stop in Cordele](https://perduesenate.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ebb829fcdaef555ddec2107e&id=ee695e9931&e=02cd889ad3)

WCTV-TV: [Senators Perdue, Ernst Bus Stop in Thomasville](https://perduesenate.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ebb829fcdaef555ddec2107e&id=3c37d918c4&e=02cd889ad3)

GRIFFIN DAILY NEWS: [Perdue fires up supporters at rally](https://perduesenate.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ebb829fcdaef555ddec2107e&id=114411967d&e=02cd889ad3)**

**_Facebook has banned us from advertising. We are counting on YOU to pass this message along to your friends and family. _**

---
| _**In the News**_
---
| **JONI ERNST→**
_“I’ll tell you, every single one of those days that I was in uniform, I was also striving to serve my Creator, God. And so for [Warnock] to tell me that because I served in uniform I could not serve God, you know, is outrageous and offensive.” _
Listen to Senator Ernst’s full discussion with Hugh Hewitt [here](https://perduesenate.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ebb829fcdaef555ddec2107e&id=f42b588428&e=02cd889ad3).
_ _
**RAND PAUL →**
_“I hope that libertarian voters if they’re listening will say, ‘You know what, now is a choice of letting Washington, D.C. become a state. It’s a choice of whether or not the taxes go up and corporations flee abroad to get lower taxes again.’ So I think there are a lot of reasons why the people who voted out of principle for the libertarian candidate this time around ought to think about you know what do I really want AOC and Bernie and these far left candidates to be running the country?” _
Listen to Senator Paul’s conversation with Tim Bryant [here](https://perduesenate.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ebb829fcdaef555ddec2107e&id=cf52f97417&e=02cd889ad3).
_ _
**MARSHA BLACKBURN →**
_“Chuck Schumer said it all. ‘If we take Georgia’ - not win Georgia, if we take Georgia - ‘we change America...’ It is imperative that everybody votes. If you like the Trump tax cuts that you got in 2017, if you want to keep your own health insurance, your own doctor, if you do not want to see the Green New Deal implemented, if you do not want D.C. to have statehood, or Puerto Rico to have statehood, or felons to have the right to vote, you need to go to the polls and vote.”_
Watch Senator Blackburn’s interview on Fox & Friends First [here](https://perduesenate.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ebb829fcdaef555ddec2107e&id=3d02ff1cc7&e=02cd889ad3).
_ _
**HUGH HEWITT →**
Yesterday in the [Washington Post](https://perduesenate.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ebb829fcdaef555ddec2107e&id=c2b5b0ad3f&e=02cd889ad3), Hugh Hewitt laid out what’s at stake if Democrats take the Senate.
_Liberals and leftists must have been chagrined by Schumer’s all-too-revealing exultation as he recently celebrated Joe Biden’s election victory, “Now we take Georgia, then we change America!” If Schumer wins Senate control, he can indeed “change America” by beginning the undoing of the capstone achievement of McConnell’s steady, disciplined six years as majority leader: confirmation of three Supreme Court justices and 53 appeals court judges.
...
The left side of the Democratic Senate caucus wants to end the supermajority requirement for legislation as well. These are the same folks who want to increase the number of seats on the Supreme Court, “packing” it to guarantee that their radical agenda is realized. With Schumer as majority leader, this too would be on the menu.
...
If Perdue and Loeffler lose the Georgia runoffs, and Schumer takes charge of the Senate, the federal judiciary will begin a leftward lurch with each Biden nominee confirmed by a simple majority. If Perdue or Loeffler wins, Biden will be more inclined to nominate moderate jurists, not hard-left “living Constitution” enthusiasts certain to be rejected._
**Read the rest of Hewitt’s op-ed[here](https://perduesenate.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ebb829fcdaef555ddec2107e&id=9552c9a5b1&e=02cd889ad3). **

**Chip in[HERE](https://perduesenate.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ebb829fcdaef555ddec2107e&id=55fff5e29b&e=02cd889ad3) to DEFEND THE MAJORITY!**

name: Steve Chabot
subject: Losing Afghanistan?
body:
No images? [Click here](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-e-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-v/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-r/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-y/)
### [Losing Afghanistan?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-j/)
It was announced recently that US troop levels in Afghanistan will soon be reduced from 4500 to 2500. I am concerned that this is a mistake and in this week's blog I explain why. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-t/)
# Previous Blog Posts
[](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-i/)
### [Suck It Up?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-d/)
Whoopi thinks conservatives like you and me just need to "suck it up." Here's what I think about that. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-h/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-k/)
### [T](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-u/)[he Blue Wave that Wasn't](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-o/)
The election has passed and the dust has settled, however who won the presidency is far from settled. In this week's blog, I discuss this election quagmire our country is confronting and something most mainstream media is ignoring, the Democrats' and pollster promised "blue wave" failed to materialize. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-b/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-n/)
### [Thank You](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-p/)
Thank you for bestowing upon me the honor of representing you in Congress for another two years. This week I briefly discuss last night's election and reveal some surprising facts. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-x/) [Donate](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-m/) [Get a Yard Sign](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-c/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-q/) | [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-a/)| [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-f/)| [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juihhldk-kujkulhluu-z/)

name: David Perdue
subject: RUNOFF - WE NEED YOUR HELP!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Perdue for Senate
body:
****

We need to hit the ground running.
__****

**BREAKING: GEORGIA RUNOFF ELECTION TO DECIDE SENATE MAJORITY**

Peter,

We're officially heading into a runoff, and **we need to hit the ground running.[ This race will decide the Senate Majority](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/56934299:sntBknKNG:m:1:2247059263:72A288E44DB632EB6DD55EB75EE3F44F:r)**

First, I want to thank you for all the support leading up to this moment. Georgia's election results proved that conservatives across Georgia are behind us and our commitment to change the direction of this country for the better. After months of desperate attacks from my opponent and his special interest allies, conservative voters still turned out in force to send a message to the liberal Democrats:_that you stand with me, and together we will protect our Georgia values and way of life._

It has been a long, hard fight, and I thank you from the bottom of my heart for being on the frontlines with me, Peter.

**But, this fight isn 't over yet, Peter.** We have one last chance to SAVE the Republican Senate Majority and Georgia is our final line of defense against complete Democrat control of the US Congress.

>From here on out, all eyes will be on Georgia, and **we must stand together to show that radical liberals cannot take over our great state.**

The future of Georgia•and the United States•depends on this runoff election, and I know I can count on principled conservatives like you, Peter, to protect Georgia from the radical socialist agenda. **We are the** **last line of defense against Chuck Schumer, Nancy Pelosi, and Socialist Jon Ossoff. **

_**We have been MASSIVELY outspent throughout this race, and we will be again if we don't have your IMMEDIATE support. **_

**[Chip in NOW to keep Georgia Red!](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/56934300:sntBknKNG:m:1:2247059263:72A288E44DB632EB6DD55EB75EE3F44F:r)

[Express Donate $250](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/56934301:sntBknKNG:m:1:2247059263:72A288E44DB632EB6DD55EB75EE3F44F:r)

[Express Donate $100](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/56934302:sntBknKNG:m:1:2247059263:72A288E44DB632EB6DD55EB75EE3F44F:r)

[Express Donate $50](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/56934303:sntBknKNG:m:1:2247059263:72A288E44DB632EB6DD55EB75EE3F44F:r)

[Express Donate $25](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/56934304:sntBknKNG:m:1:2247059263:72A288E44DB632EB6DD55EB75EE3F44F:r)

[Express Donate $10](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/56934305:sntBknKNG:m:1:2247059263:72A288E44DB632EB6DD55EB75EE3F44F:r)**

You--and all of my team's supporters--have been working hard since the start of this campaign, and haven't let up for a moment. We've got until January 5th in this leg of the race, and we need to put 100% of our energy and resources into _defeating_ Jon Ossoff. We cannot allow the Left to steal this seat and gain control of the Senate, destroying our great nation and everything it stands for.

We need to show every left-wing special interest donor that we will not let them hand over Georgia to the liberal mob.

**I refuse to sit by and let Georgia become ground zero for the Socialist movement, with higher taxes and defunded police departments. I am counting on your support to protect the state we know and love.**

[**Any amount, no matter how big or small, will go a long way in these crucial next few days in showing the far-Left radical mob that we will not back down.**](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/56934299:sntBknKNG:m:1:2247059263:72A288E44DB632EB6DD55EB75EE3F44F:r)

The future of Georgia will be determined on January 5th. You have a choice between Chuck Schumer, Stacey Abrams, and Jon Ossoff's radical Socialist agenda and our America First agenda.

[**Chip in today to Keep Georgia Red!**](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/56934300:sntBknKNG:m:1:2247059263:72A288E44DB632EB6DD55EB75EE3F44F:r)

Thank you for all your support thus far. Let's finish this fight and keep Georgia red.

Thank you,

David Perdue

[****](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/56934300:sntBknKNG:m:1:2247059263:72A288E44DB632EB6DD55EB75EE3F44F:r)

Paid for by Perdue for Senate

name: Mitch McConnell
subject: Should we give up?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MCCONNELL SENATE COMMITTEE
body:

The choice is yours. But if you are truly committed to fighting for our Majority and our conservative agenda, we need your support IMMEDIATELY, which is why we've UNLOCKED 500% MATCHING.

name: Mike Kelly For Congress
subject: A Blessed Thanksgiving to You and Yours!
body:
[](https://www.trailblz.info/kellymike/gateway/1.172833.aspx)
Dear Peter,
As I sit here this Thanksgiving morning, I think about everything that I am grateful for - a beautiful wife, wonderful children and precious grandchildren. Truly a life filled of God's blessings. Most especially - you. Thank you for your continued support and friendship. Thank you for always continuing the fight for freedom and for never taking a play off or a day off. I admire each and every one of you. I hope you and yours have a great start to the holiday season!
Wishing you a very happy, healthy and blessed Thanksgiving!
**_Thank you for your continued support and God Bless America!_**
My best,
_Mike_

subject: Who is Jon Ossoff?

body:
The stakes in this runoff election could not be higher, and today I want to introduce you to my radical opponent, Jon Ossoff.
Friend,

The stakes in this runoff election could not be higher, and today I want to introduce you to my radical opponent, Jon Ossoff.

Let's look at the facts:
* **He is nothing but a puppet of the Left.** Jon Ossoff is a 33-year old wannabe career politician with no real-world accomplishments. He is a vehicle for the extreme left to advance their toxic agenda.
* **He is incredibly radical.** He is supported by Communist Party U.S.A., has been endorsed by Bernie Sanders, and previously engaged in business with a media company partly owned by the Chinese Communist government and then tried to hide it from the people of Georgia.
* **He is an economic nightmare.** He opposed Republican tax cuts and instead supports a plan that will drastically raise taxes. He opposed the Paycheck Protection Program that saved 1.5 million Georgia jobs, even though his own father used a loan to save his company.
* **He supports the defund the police movement.** In an interview with WAOK, Ossoff said that "funding has to be on the line" for police departments- the very definition of defunding the police. He also refused to sign the pledge to support law enforcement.
* **He doesn 't actually care about Georgia.** He has offered Georgia's agricultural community NOTHING in terms of policy, assistance, or even attention. He supports a radical healthcare system. His public option means that 40+ rural Georgia hospitals could close.

He has offered Georgia's agricultural community NOTHING in terms of policy, assistance, or even attention. He supports a radical healthcare system. His public option means that 40+ rural Georgia hospitals could close. == == == == == == ==** Thank you, David Perdue U.S. Senator for Georgia Paid for by Perdue for Senate | | Perdue for Senate | PO Box 12077, Atlanta, GA 30355 | | Sent by mailto:action@standupforliberty.net powered by

name: Steve Chabot Press
subject: ICYMI: Let's put politics aside and work together for the people
body:
I want to thank the people of Ohio’s 1st Congressional District for once again placing their confidence in me to represent them in Washington. I’m fir
No images? [Click here](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-e-juiktiz-kujkulhluu-d/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juiktiz-kujkulhluu-r/)
## **Chabot: Let's put politics aside and work together for the people**
Steve Chabot, Opinion contributor[Cincinnati.com](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juiktiz-kujkulhluu-y/), November 25 2020 I want to thank the people of Ohio's 1st Congressional District for once again placing their confidence in me to represent them in Washington. I'm firmly committed to representing ALL of the people of Cincinnati and Hamilton and Warren counties to the best of my ability, and to continue to strengthen our communities and protect our shared values.With the election behind us, it's time for Republicans and Democrats to put politics aside, and come together to solve our problems. We should start by providing additional Paycheck Protection Program (PPP) relief for our small businesses and their workers - many of whom are still struggling to survive the pandemic.When the pandemic first hit, as ranking member of the House Small Business Committee, I worked closely with Democratic Chairwoman Nydia Velazquez of New York and our counterparts in the Senate, to craft and implement the critically-needed bipartisan PPP program. It's been a tremendous success, both for our region, and for the country.The 1st Congressional District received more PPP funding, nearly $3 billion; more small businesses received loans, over 17,000; and most importantly, more jobs were saved, 256,000, than any other district in Ohio.Over the past several months, I've visited more than 70 small businesses and non-profits in our community that received PPP loans, to listen to their concerns and get input on how we can improve the program.For example, I visited Schmoe's Collision on the west side. The owner of the business, Dan Wenger, who'd been a pillar of the community, had recently passed away. Dan's wife, Susan Wenger, and manager, Barry Burriss, were trying to keep the business going. They told me the PPP loan literally saved the business and the jobs of 18 people. I've been working in Congress to pass legislation permitting a second round of PPP loans and save additional jobs. So many small businesses continue to struggle due to COVID-19, and I'd like to let them know that help is on the way. In order to accomplish this, and pass other commonsense measures to benefit the American people during these challenging times, it's going to take bipartisanship - Republicans and Democrats working together. I learned years ago that's the way to get things done. Whenever I offer legislation, I always get a Democrat cosponsor. It's one of the reasons the last two times the University of Virginia and Vanderbilt have done studies on Congress, I've been ranked among the Top Ten most effective Members of Congress. Working together works.I'd like to continue delivering effective representation to the people of the 1st Congressional District. That's why whatever happens with redistricting this time around, I will run again in 2022. The challenges we face as a community and as a nation are too important to back away from the fight. I intend to continue to utilize my experience to make sure our community remains a great place to live, work and raise a family. | [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juiktiz-kujkulhluu-j/)| [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juiktiz-kujkulhluu-t/)| [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juiktiz-kujkulhluu-i/)
---|---|---
Chabot for Congress, P.O. Box 54461, Cincinnati, OH 45254
You are receiving this email because of your connection to our cause. If you receive this email in error, please use the unsubscribe link below and remove yourself from future mailings.

name: Kevin Hern
subject: Happy Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Hern For Congress
body:
I want to wish you and your loved ones all the best today.
Dear Peter, I wanted to drop a quick note to wish you and your family a wonderful holiday today. It’s certainly been a challenging year. Our families, our small businesses, our schools, our churches and community groups – we've all been impacted. Yet we have much to be thankful for as we celebrate Thanksgiving 2020 and look ahead to the future and continue to persevere. Today, I am thankful for my wonderful family and for good health. I’m thankful for the freedom and liberty that have made America the greatest nation in the history of the world. I’m thankful for friends like you, who’ve stepped up to help defend all we hold dear. Most of all I’m thankful for our risen Savior and the hope that He brings to our lives. Thank you, Peter, for being part of the team. I hope you and your loved ones enjoy a wonderful Thanksgiving today. \- Kevin id:2020-10-15-19:41:18:515t Paid For By Hern For Congress
---

This email was sent by: Kevin Hern
9521-B Riverside Pkwy, #350, Tulsa, OK, 74137

name: Jenny Beth Martin, Tea Party Patriots Action
subject: My Thanksgiving wish for you and your family
body:
_The Lord is my strength and my shield; in him my heart trusts, and I am helped; my heart exults, and with my song I give thanks to him._
Psalm 28:7 (English Standard Version)
What a year, eh? It would seem that after everything our nation has experienced in 2020, it would be harder to rejoice and give thanks – but I don’t believe that to be the case. Even in the midst of trials and tribulation and suffering, there are blessings for which we can be grateful. In fact, there are blessings for which we should be grateful, despite the sorrow and injustice we see around us, and even as we see government officials prohibiting families from gathering together to celebrate – in a supposedly free country. Cultivating a grateful heart, no matter the world’s circumstances, ensures that we do the right things for the right reasons, and that we are not overcome with the same bitterness and hatred that we have witnessed not just this year, but for the last four years. Sadly, it is too easy to ignore our blessings and focus only on the brokenness of our world. I implore you to reject this path, and encourage you, even though it is difficult sometimes, to find the true, the good, and the beautiful in life. This is also why history is so important, and why we must work to ensure future generations of Americans know real history. It is history that reminds us that people, and our country, have gone through hard times before. And yet, the people who came before were still thankful, as we can be as well. I am including excerpts from the Thanksgiving Proclamations from George Washington and Abraham Lincoln below my signature, because firstly, they are beautiful pieces of our history. And secondly, they remind us that our nation has faced upheaval and division before, and yet we triumphed over it. When Washington gave his, it was just six years after the Revolutionary War had ended; Lincoln gave his in the middle of the Civil War. **Therefore, it is my fervent wish this Thanksgiving for you and your family, that you enjoy each other – in person, if at all possible; that you share your love and gratitude for one another; and that you see and appreciate all of the blessings in your life, big or small.** I wish you good health, good food, and the confidence that comes from knowing our truest joy and hope do not rest in this world. **And, it is my fervent prayer that if you one of our older members who is forced to be alone this Thanksgiving, you know you are loved and appreciated; I pray you know you are not alone.** Wishing you a happy and blessed Thanksgiving, Jenny Beth Martin **Excerpt from George Washington’s Thanksgiving Proclamation
October 3, 17891** Now therefore I do recommend and assign Thursday the 26th day of November next to be devoted by the People of these States to the service of that great and glorious Being, who is the beneficent Author of all the good that was, that is, or that will be—That we may then all unite in rendering unto him our sincere and humble thanks—for his kind care and protection of the People of this Country previous to their becoming a Nation—for the signal and manifold mercies, and the favorable interpositions of his Providence which we experienced in the course and conclusion of the late war—for the great degree of tranquillity, union, and plenty, which we have since enjoyed—for the peaceable and rational manner, in which we have been enabled to establish constitutions of government for our safety and happiness, and particularly the national One now lately instituted—for the civil and religious liberty with which we are blessed; and the means we have of acquiring and diffusing useful knowledge; and in general for all the great and various favors which he hath been pleased to confer upon us. And also that we may then unite in most humbly offering our prayers and supplications to the great Lord and Ruler of Nations and beseech him to pardon our national and other transgressions—to enable us all, whether in public or private stations, to perform our several and relative duties properly and punctually—to render our national government a blessing to all the people, by constantly being a Government of wise, just, and constitutional laws, discreetly and faithfully executed and obeyed—to protect and guide all Sovereigns and Nations (especially such as have shewn kindness unto us) and to bless them with good government, peace, and concord- **Excerpt from Abraham Lincoln’s Thanksgiving Proclamation** **October 3, 1863 2** I do therefore invite my fellow citizens in every part of the United States, and also those who are at sea and those who are sojourning in foreign lands, to set apart and observe the last Thursday of November next, as a day of Thanksgiving and Praise to our beneficent Father who dwelleth in the Heavens. And I recommend to them that while offering up the ascriptions justly due to Him for such singular deliverances and blessings, they do also, with humble penitence for our national perverseness and disobedience, commend to His tender care all those who have become widows, orphans, mourners or sufferers in the lamentable civil strife in which we are unavoidably engaged, and fervently implore the interposition of the Almighty Hand to heal the wounds of the nation and to restore it as soon as may be consistent with the Divine purposes to the full enjoyment of peace, harmony, tranquillity and Union. | |
---

Even in the midst of trials and tribulation and suffering, there are blessings for which we can be grateful. In fact, there are blessings for which we should be grateful, despite the sorrow and injustice we see around us, and even as we see government officials prohibiting families from gathering together to celebrate – in a supposedly free country. Cultivating a grateful heart, no matter the world’s circumstances, ensures that we do the right things for the right reasons, and that we are not overcome with the same bitterness and hatred that we have witnessed not just this year, but for the last four years. Sadly, it is too easy to ignore our blessings and focus only on the brokenness of our world. I implore you to reject this path, and encourage you, even though it is difficult sometimes, to find the true, the good, and the beautiful in life. This is also why history is so important, and why we must work to ensure future generations of Americans know real history. It is history that reminds us that people, and our country, have gone through hard times before. And yet, the people who came before were still thankful, as we can be as well. I am including excerpts from the Thanksgiving Proclamations from George Washington and Abraham Lincoln below my signature, because firstly, they are beautiful pieces of our history. And secondly, they remind us that our nation has faced upheaval and division before, and yet we triumphed over it. When Washington gave his, it was just six years after the Revolutionary War had ended; Lincoln gave his in the middle of the Civil War. Therefore, it is my fervent wish this Thanksgiving for you and your family, that you enjoy each other – in person, if at all possible; that you share your love and gratitude for one another; and that you see and appreciate all of the blessings in your life, big or small. I wish you good health, good food, and the confidence that comes from knowing our truest joy and hope do not rest in this world. And, it is my fervent prayer that if you one of our older members who is forced to be alone this Thanksgiving, you know you are loved and appreciated; I pray you know you are not alone. Wishing you a happy and blessed Thanksgiving, Jenny Beth Martin Excerpt from George Washington’s Thanksgiving Proclamation October 3, 17891 Now therefore I do recommend and assign Thursday the 26th day of November next to be devoted by the People of these States to the service of that great and glorious Being, who is the beneficent Author of all the good that was, that is, or that will be—That we may then all unite in rendering unto him our sincere and humble thanks—for his kind care and protection of the People of this Country previous to their becoming a Nation—for the signal and manifold mercies, and the favorable interpositions of his Providence which we experienced in the course and conclusion of the late war—for the great degree of tranquillity, union, and plenty, which we have since enjoyed—for the peaceable and rational manner, in which we have been enabled to establish constitutions of government for our safety and happiness, and particularly the national One now lately instituted—for the civil and religious liberty with which we are blessed; and the means we have of acquiring and diffusing useful knowledge; and in general for all the great and various favors which he hath been pleased to confer upon us. And also that we may then unite in most humbly offering our prayers and supplications to the great Lord and Ruler of Nations and beseech him to pardon our national and other transgressions—to enable us all, whether in public or private stations, to perform our several and relative duties properly and punctually—to render our national government a blessing to all the people, by constantly being a Government of wise, just, and constitutional laws, discreetly and faithfully executed and obeyed—to protect and guide all Sovereigns and Nations (especially such as have shewn kindness unto us) and to bless them with good government, peace, and concord- Excerpt from Abraham Lincoln’s Thanksgiving Proclamation October 3, 1863 2 I do therefore invite my fellow citizens in every part of the United States, and also those who are at sea and those who are sojourning in foreign lands, to set apart and observe the last Thursday of November next, as a day of Thanksgiving and Praise to our beneficent Father who dwelleth in the Heavens. And I recommend to them that while offering up the ascriptions justly due to Him for such singular deliverances and blessings, they do also, with humble penitence for our national perverseness and disobedience, commend to His tender care all those who have become widows, orphans, mourners or sufferers in the lamentable civil strife in which we are unavoidably engaged, and fervently implore the interposition of the Almighty Hand to heal the wounds of the nation and to restore it as soon as may be consistent with the Divine purposes to the full enjoyment of peace, harmony, tranquillity and Union. | | Tea Party Patriots Action operates as a social welfare organization under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code. Contributions to Tea Party Patriots Action are not deductible as charitable contributions for income tax purposes. © 2020 , All rights reserved. 80 M St SE, First Floor, Washington, D.C. 20003

name: Nicole Malliotakis
subject: I am thankful for you
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY NICOLE FOR NEW YORK (FEDERAL)
body:
Friend, On this Thanksgiving Day, I wanted to take a minute to express how thankful I am to live in this great country where we have freedom and opportunity, and to have supporters like you who share my values and love for America. I am humbled to have been chosen to be your voice in Congress and will take every opportunity to fight to preserve our liberties and the American Dream.
| | | | Friend,

On this Thanksgiving Day, I wanted to take a minute to express how thankful I am to live in this great country where we have freedom and opportunity, and to have supporters like you who share my values and love for America.

I am humbled to have been chosen to be your voice in Congress and will take every opportunity to fight to preserve our liberties and the American Dream.

I know that this year has been a difficult one for all of us but, together, we will get through this stronger than ever before because that is what we are about. We take care of our neighbors and give thanks for our healthy families and our great nation.

From my family to yours, have a happy, healthy, and blessed Thanksgiving.

Sincerely,

---
| |
---
| | Nicole Malliotakis
Congresswoman-Elect, NY-11

---
| | |
---
| | | PAID FOR BY NICOLE FOR NEW YORK (FEDERAL)

name: Newt Gingrich
subject: If we lose Georgia, we lose the Senate.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Victory Action Fund
body:
****

name: Mary Mancini
subject: A Thanksgiving Note
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/77134/images/Thanksgiving%20-%20EDITS.png]
Dear PeterOn behalf of the TNDP staff, I want to wish you a very Happy Thanksgiving. In these uncertain times, while our lives have changed in ways most of us couldn’t imagine, your generosity and unwavering support of Tennessee Democrats has been remarkable. We are thankful for you.
I’d also like to say how thankful I am for the hard work and commitment of the TNDP staff - Kris Murphy, Maria Brewer, Sandra Sepulveda, Evan Latt, Emily Cupples, Julio Salazar, and Kendra Lee. This is a team that really showed up this election and I couldn’t be more proud of the work we did. I’m also thankful for the incredibly talented slate of Democratic candidates we had this cycle. It was truly a privilege working and mobilizing on their behalf.
Lastly, though the holiday will look very different this year, may you and yours feel the love that surrounds us and sense the shared human experience that connects us. Happy Thanksgiving.
Thanks again,[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/77134/images/signature2.png]--
Mary Mancini
Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party
PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR. GEETA MCMILLAN, TREASURER NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE. CONTRIBUTIONS OR GIFTS TO TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY ARE NOT TAX DEDUCTIBLE.

319 Plus Park Blvd
Nashville, TN 37217
United States

name: Seth Moulton
subject: This Thanksgiving is different
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Serve America PAC
body:
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/21918335/262589372/-631061264?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNGNmYjM5Ni1mZjJmLWViMTEtOWZiNC0wMDE1NWQ0M2IyY2QiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dQgsO_Spe-lxfokVXsFhGOfmS8QfYnjNvdkJZGsSiMY=&emci=c1ec2a89-292f-eb11-9fb4-00155d43b2cd&emdi=34cfb396-ff2f-eb11-9fb4-00155d43b2cd&ceid=194769) Friend, This is the first Thanksgiving in my life I don’t want to spend with my wonderful, loving parents. I made it clear they were uninvited because the risk is too great. But I’m also thankful they are alive and well (though lonely). For over 250,000 American families, the empty chairs at their tables this year are a grim reminder of the devastating toll that COVID-19 continues to take on American life. In this extraordinary year, it may be difficult to feel gratitude, so I want to share a quick story: _When my first tour in Iraq finally ended, we got off the plane and piled onto school buses to drive back to base, and I started taking photos. Suddenly I caught myself and realized I had a dozen photos of random American homes in a nondescript California suburb. What was once normal seemed extraordinary—truly beautiful—after so long away. Without even noticing it at first, I had a newfound appreciation for the everyday things we take for granted in America._ One of the many truths I know about Americans is that we are resilient. We’ll get through this pandemic and come out stronger than before. My hope for us is that, when we finally do, we have a renewed gratitude for the daily parts of life we used to take for granted. And until that time, I hope you are able to find grace in small moments of kindness and fellowship, whatever those look like for you this year. I’m grateful for my family, especially because Emmy is going to become a big sister in a few months! I’m grateful for all of you, for your hard work and commitment to this team. And I’m grateful that in 2021, we will restore empathy, dignity, and competency to the White House. In the face of adversity, let’s count our blessings. Happy Thanksgiving, Seth | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/21918336/262589373/1848844371?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNGNmYjM5Ni1mZjJmLWViMTEtOWZiNC0wMDE1NWQ0M2IyY2QiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dQgsO_Spe-lxfokVXsFhGOfmS8QfYnjNvdkJZGsSiMY=&emci=c1ec2a89-292f-eb11-9fb4-00155d43b2cd&emdi=34cfb396-ff2f-eb11-9fb4-00155d43b2cd&ceid=194769) | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/21918337/262589374/-1745464800?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNGNmYjM5Ni1mZjJmLWViMTEtOWZiNC0wMDE1NWQ0M2IyY2QiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dQgsO_Spe-lxfokVXsFhGOfmS8QfYnjNvdkJZGsSiMY=&emci=c1ec2a89-292f-eb11-9fb4-00155d43b2cd&emdi=34cfb396-ff2f-eb11-9fb4-00155d43b2cd&ceid=194769) | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/21918338/262589375/341442065?hl=en&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNGNmYjM5Ni1mZjJmLWViMTEtOWZiNC0wMDE1NWQ0M2IyY2QiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dQgsO_Spe-lxfokVXsFhGOfmS8QfYnjNvdkJZGsSiMY=&emci=c1ec2a89-292f-eb11-9fb4-00155d43b2cd&emdi=34cfb396-ff2f-eb11-9fb4-00155d43b2cd&ceid=194769) | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/21918339/262589376/-368236352?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNGNmYjM5Ni1mZjJmLWViMTEtOWZiNC0wMDE1NWQ0M2IyY2QiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dQgsO_Spe-lxfokVXsFhGOfmS8QfYnjNvdkJZGsSiMY=&emci=c1ec2a89-292f-eb11-9fb4-00155d43b2cd&emdi=34cfb396-ff2f-eb11-9fb4-00155d43b2cd&ceid=194769)

name: Melissa Hortman
subject: Thankful
disclaimer_text: paid for by the DFL House Caucus
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/QkyVabmK05eUSPz6OWIebluvfN_I8FOBmuAhMIwuk6o=/xMuo7t5sthmvX3QrjlH-p7qAaIquicvUMjGsdNlgpdU=?refcode=112620)
| | Friend, This last year has been challenging, but there is still so much to be thankful for. From protecting our House DFL Majority to governing around our shared values, we couldn't have done it without you. While many Minnesotans give thanks today, we also acknowledge the pain and trauma of this holiday for Indigenous Minnesotans and recognize our responsibility to build a Minnesota that works better for everyone. My heart goes out to those who have lost a loved one to COVID-19 and have an empty seat at their table this year. Please stay home, avoid large gatherings, and help us prevent this public health crisis from getting worse. I am so thankful for your support and engagement. From my family to yours, I wish you a very happy socially distanced Thanksgiving. Thank you, Melissa Hortman Speaker Minnesota House of Representatives _ _ [**Donate**](https://act.myngp.com/el/QkyVabmK05eUSPz6OWIebluvfN_I8FOBmuAhMIwuk6o=/xMuo7t5sthmvX3QrjlH-p7qAaIquicvUMjGsdNlgpdU=?refcode=112620)

name: David Perdue
subject: Who is John Ossoff?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Perdue for Senate
body:
****

The stakes in this runoff election could not be higher, and today I want to introduce you to my radical opponent, Jon Ossoff.
Peter,

The stakes in this runoff election could not be higher, and today I want to introduce you to my radical opponent, Jon Ossoff.

Let's look at the facts:
* **He is nothing but a puppet of the Left.** Jon Ossoff is a 33-year old wannabe career politician with no real-world accomplishments. He is a vehicle for the extreme left to advance their toxic agenda.
* **He is incredibly radical.** He is supported by Communist Party U.S.A., has been endorsed by Bernie Sanders, and previously engaged in business with a media company partly owned by the Chinese Communist government and then tried to hide it from the people of Georgia.
* **He is an economic nightmare.** He opposed Republican tax cuts and instead supports a plan that will drastically raise taxes. He opposed the Paycheck Protection Program that saved 1.5 million Georgia jobs, even though his own father used a loan to save his company.
* **He supports the defund the police movement.** In an interview with WAOK, Ossoff said that "funding has to be on the line" for police departments- the very definition of defunding the police. He also refused to sign the pledge to support law enforcement.
* **He doesn 't actually care about Georgia.** He has offered Georgia's agricultural community NOTHING in terms of policy, assistance, or even attention. He supports a radical healthcare system. His public option means that 40+ rural Georgia hospitals could close.

[******Georgia deserves better than Jon Ossoff, and today I need to ask you to rush an emergency contribution to guarantee that he NEVER represents our great state**](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/56938788:s57wGnKNP:m:1:2247059263:36ABD3FB1F113FC8581CB19AB4C6FBAB:r)

name: Mike Thompson
subject: Have a happy and safe Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress [unsubscribe](https://email
body:
|
---
| | Peter,

Wishing everyone a safe and happy Thanksgiving. Janet and I both consider this one of our favorite holidays, full of good food and great company. Even though this year we are only cooking for the two of us, we are still looking forward to talking with our kids and grandkids later this afternoon.

Though it's hard for us all to be celebrating differently and without our family and loved ones, this is a really important part of keeping us and our communities safe and healthy as Coronavirus cases spike.

Today, we are taking stock of all that we have. I continue to be thankful for our essential workers who risk their lives everyday to keep us safe and I am thankful that my family is safe and healthy. And I am forever humbled to have the opportunity to serve you and our district in Congress.

Please continue to follow the CDC’s detailed list of recommendations so that everyone can stay safe and healthy. [**_Click here for best practices on how you can protect your family and your health during this holiday season._**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/21921549/262604402/1030256748?CDC_AA_refVal=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.cdc.gov%2Fcoronavirus%2F2019-ncov%2Fprepare%2Findex.html&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDQ0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2OWU0MWUwMy0wZDMwLWViMTEtOWZiNC0wMDE1NWQ0M2IyY2QiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4Ju2L7KUfFvzPHancYExcr2btAu4z0_1bvGoCiG6WTY=&emci=a2bf84e3-a12e-eb11-9fb4-00155d43b2cd&emdi=69e41e03-0d30-eb11-9fb4-00155d43b2cd&ceid=2678920)

Happy Thanksgiving!

Mike Thompson

name: =?utf-8?B?TGluZGEgU8OhbmNoZXo=?=
subject: Wishing you a healthy and safe Thanksgiving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stand with Sánchez
body:
The holiday season has snuck up on us once again!
After a difficult year, it's natural to want to celebrate Thanksgiving with our friends and family. **_However_** , with COVID-19 cases on the rise across the country -- including here in Southern California -- now is the time to prioritize the health and safety of our communities by following COVID-19 safety guidelines.
That includes staying at home when possible, keeping our gatherings small, and wearing a mask outdoors.
**[Find more information about how to keep you and your loved ones safe and healthy over the holidays>>](https://act.myngp.com/el/iD-sHfYGDQZfKLuBnuGXJNmLpQWBKxboWm_XO0A6gTk=/Z9rXIduj9dk2BDWAEXTnskJvcP8kLRz2FmQjSX2XwUo=)**
As someone who looks forward to holiday get-togethers all year long, I understand how disappointing it feels to not be able to gather with friends and family this year. But, by social distancing and practicing caution, we are helping preserve future celebrations with our loved ones.
Instead, I encourage each of you to use today as a time for reflection -- what or who is it that has helped you get through this year? Where, how, and with who will you celebrate next year, when we hopefully find ourselves out of this crisis?
This year has been hard on us all, but there is still much to be thankful for. I, for one, am thankful for you. Your support keeps me fighting for the working families of CA-38; a responsibility that I will _never_ take for granted.
With that, I want to wish you a safe, healthy, and happy Thanksgiving and holiday season.
From my family to yours,
_\-- Linda S anchez_
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/iD-sHfYGDQZfKLuBnuGXJNmLpQWBKxboWm_XO0A6gTk=/M7MQWGyoA9-h7tb--fG12O1CXmu75f6nsDfsJPsSV20=?refcode=X_112620_Thanksgiving)

* * *

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Happy Thanksgiving, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
| [ ](https://act.myngp.com/el/lufvTiWjU3zW5kTdplu91QORNcjLEQYHPgVu-RC8ZXA=/_-9hp7WlBsQsYvk3U285dSlCwaPC5ClERA6i-aouw-0=?refcode=BLU-SPAN-20201126-1-EM&amount=25)
---
| Peter,

Each Thanksgiving, my father insists that we circle the dinner table, each taking stock of all that we are thankful for.

This year, my parents, my sisters, and our families will join via Zoom to celebrate, and I suspect that we will follow the usual tradition, so in the spirit of gratitude, I would like to share my list with you all.

I am thankful for the technology that will ensure my family continues our annual tradition, much to the chagrin of a few family members!

I am thankful for the health of my family and friends, and acutely aware of how hard this Thanksgiving will be for those who have lost a loved one to the pandemic or who are struggling because of job loss or a faltering business.

I am thankful for the sacrifices and commitment of our nurses, doctors, healthcare workers, public health officials, and scientists, as well as those participating in the COVID-19 vaccine trials.

I am thankful for our police, firefighters, first responders, and teachers as they continue to ensure the safety and learning of our communities.

I am thankful for the frontline workers, grocery store clerks, and truck drivers who kept our supply chains open and continue to ensure we can have a holiday.

I am thankful for the food banks and safety-net organizations that are lifesaving during this pandemic, and I am grateful to our neighbors who support them with their time or treasure.

Lastly, I am thankful for the responsibility of serving the people of our district in Congress. As so many are hurting across our country, I have been working daily for a negotiated COVID-19 deal. In my meetings, calls, and Zooms with Senators and House members, I am singularly focused on ensuring the relief our neighbors, our local businesses, and economy need. I know a deal cannot come fast enough, and I am thankful for the ability to be fighting for it on behalf of my constituents.

Thank you,

_Abigail_
---

* * *
| | [ ](https://act.myngp.com/el/lufvTiWjU3zW5kTdplu91QORNcjLEQYHPgVu-RC8ZXA=/_-9hp7WlBsQsYvk3U285dSlCwaPC5ClERA6i-aouw-0=?refcode=BLU-SPAN-20201126-1-EM&amount=25)

---
| Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
---

PO Box 3121
Glen Allen VA 23058 United States
Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat, former CIA officer, and representative for Virginia's 7th Congressional District. She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. To learn more, please visit [AbigailSpanberger.com](https://act.myngp.com/el/lufvTiWjU3zW5kTdplu91QORNcjLEQYHPgVu-RC8ZXA=/fUac6p6F8wOGakepTGcLWesKg7tAW-spw5tFeYsnDYs=).

Thank you, Abigail * | | | Paid for by Spanberger for Congress Spanberger for Congress PO Box 3121 Glen Allen VA 23058 United States Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat, former CIA officer, and representative for Virginia's 7th Congressional District. name: Governor Eric Holcomb
subject: Happy Thanksgiving, from mine to yours
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
Like many things in this unprecedented year, Thanksgiving dinners across our state might look a little different, but we, as Hoosiers, still have a... | |
---
| |
---|---|---
|
---
Friend \- Happy Thanksgiving!

Like many things in this unprecedented year, Thanksgiving tables across our state might look a little different today — smaller, and more reserved.

But in Indiana, we still have so much to be thankful for. Thanksgiving is a special time for Hoosiers, and while our gatherings might be smaller this year, the spirit of generosity from Hoosiers has never been larger.

I’m planning on using my family’s quieter Thanksgiving (just me and my wife, Janet, and our dog, Henry) to reflect on what’s most important, and of course, what I am most thankful for in my life — especially my family, Indiana’s first responders and medical professionals working around the clock, and Hoosiers throughout our state who continue to show the compassion and generosity Indiana is known for.

I encourage you to do the same.

Happy Thanksgiving,

Governor Eric Holcomb
---
---

name: Rob Portman
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rob Portman for Senate
body:
Jane and I wanted to wish you and your family a very Happy Thanksgiving. While
we’re all celebrating a little differently this year, I love......

Peter,

Jane and I wanted to wish you and your family a very Happy Thanksgiving.

While we’re all celebrating a little differently this year, I love
Thanksgiving because it is focused on family, great food, and allows us all the
chance to reflect on the blessings we have received. This year it is
particularly important that we look past the challenges and remember our
blessings.

Whether it’s the family members or friends we’re fortunate enough to celebrate
with, the many heroes in our midst, like the nurses and grocery store workers
during this pandemic, the men and women in uniform here and around the world
who keep us safe, or simply the fact that we are blessed to live in this great
nation... Thanksgiving provides us all with the chance to count our blessings.

Peter, I’m grateful today for your support and encouragement. Whether you’ve
trusted me with your vote, contributed to my campaign, or sent along your
thoughts and ideas, none of which you have to do, you help me do my best to
represent the great state of Ohio, and allow me to serve America and help keep
us the beacon of hope for the rest of the world. I am very grateful for you and
count you as one of my blessings.

I pray that you and your family have a happy, healthy Thanksgiving.

- Rob Portman

Paid for by Rob Portman for Senate

| | Friends,

Today is a time to reflect on the many blessings God has given us in our lives. As we celebrate our own good fortunes, may we remember to share them with those around us. Thanksgiving reminds us that our nation thrives on the individual freedom to care for one another.

Audrey and I wish you a wonderful holiday filled with family, food, and fellowship. Happy Thanksgiving!

God Bless,

Tracey

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Today, I'm thankful for you
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JAY INSLEE FOR WASHINGTON (D)
body:
| **From the Desk of
Jay Inslee** Friend, It's a very different Thanksgiving for so many of us this year -- but in spite of everything, there's still so much to be thankful for. I'm thankful for a healthy and happy family. Even if we can't be together today, I know the choices we made today will keep us happy and healthy in the months and years to come -- and that's something to celebrate. I'm thankful for the beginning of a bright new future for our country. I know that there's so much to fix in the coming months and years, but I'm confident that Joe Biden and Kamala Harris will do everything in their power to build a more just, inclusive, and safe country for all of us starting next year -- and for that, I know the Inslees and so many other families are so deeply grateful. Most of all, truly, I'm thankful for you. In a year with so much uncertainty, you stepped up to fight for the future of our climate, a safer and healthier future for our families, and a fairer and more equal country for every community. You stood up to say Black Lives Matter, science should lead the way, and truth should always prevail over misinformation. Even if we're safe, healthy, and lucky to be around our smaller holiday table, too many families are not. Trudi and I will hold them close to our hearts today and hope the work in the months to come heals the divisions and the divides that leave too many fearful and hungry today and every day. It may not seem like it, but there is still so much to be thankful for. I hope you take some time today to reflect on what you're thankful for and what you can do -- what we can all do -- to make a better world for all of us. Happy Thanksgiving, from our family to yours. Very truly yours, Jay | [DONATE TO WORLD
CENTRAL KITCHEN](https://act.jayinslee.com/go/13857?t=3&akid=8224%2E868730%2ErkkIyg)
---

name: =?utf-8?Q?Jerry?=
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
body:
| | | |
---
| **Happy Thanksgiving!**
---
| | |
---
| **Hi Friends! Happy Thanksgiving to you and your family. I pray that God's peace, blessings, providence, health, joy, and hope would be upon you and your family.

Today is the day where America celebrates Thanksgiving - not only to give thanks, but give thanks specifically to God for his amazing providence towards our lives and our great nation. From the settling at New Plymouth by the Pilgrims, to the Revolutionary War, through the Civil War, and beyond, God's providence has brought America through sometimes impossible odds. God has done it before, and I believe He will do it again. I have hope and faith for the future of America, and I thank God for all that He will do in the future.

Please join me today in giving thanks and praise to God for how he has blessed your life and America, and let's pray for our great nation together. **

**God bless you, and God bless America

-Jerry, Lisa, (& Michael) Evans**

**_Please check out my [website](https://JerryEvansIL.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5b630adf46d0f4acc3511961b&id=938cdb4c0b&e=b78e7c4d40) to learn more about me and [my vision](https://JerryEvansIL.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5b630adf46d0f4acc3511961b&id=2cc0b78096&e=b78e7c4d40) for our country, and follow me one of my social media accounts to keep up with my journey._**

clean_body: | | | | | Happy Thanksgiving! | | | | Hi Friends! Happy Thanksgiving to you and your family. I pray that God's peace, blessings, providence, health, joy, and hope would be upon you and your family. Today is the day where America celebrates Thanksgiving - not only to give thanks, but give thanks specifically to God for his amazing providence towards our lives and our great nation. From the settling at New Plymouth by the Pilgrims, to the Revolutionary War, through the Civil War, and beyond, God's providence has brought America through sometimes impossible odds. God has done it before, and I believe He will do it again. I have hope and faith for the future of America, and I thank God for all that He will do in the future. Please join me today in giving thanks and praise to God for how he has blessed your life and America, and let's pray for our great nation together. God bless you, and God bless America -Jerry, Lisa, (& Michael) Evans Please check out my to learn more about me and for our country, and follow me one of my social media accounts to keep up with my journey. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright © 2020 - Jerry Evans for Illinois, All rights reserved. Peter,
I hope you and your family are doing well, staying healthy, and enjoying our beautiful New Mexico fall season.
Although we are all celebrating the Thanksgiving holiday a bit differently this year because of COVID, this still remains a time when we all pause and reflect on the many blessings God has given us.
The past year has been especially difficult for so many in New Mexico and across the world but my hope is that through it all, we will continue to support those in need, appreciate the reminder to value health, family and friends, and focus on the strength we find in our faith.
This past year was certainly an incredible journey for my family and me, and we are so grateful that you were such a big part of it along the way. Regardless of the outcome of the election, one thing that will never change is the love we have for our state and our nation. We are so thankful for the culture, values and freedoms we hold dear and we will always continue to fight to protect them.
Krysty, Ella, Ava and I are so thankful for the blessings in our life and the many friends we’ve gained – including you.
We hope you have a wonderful Thanksgiving holiday.
---

Although we are all celebrating the Thanksgiving holiday a bit differently this year because of COVID, this still remains a time when we all pause and reflect on the many blessings God has given us. The past year has been especially difficult for so many in New Mexico and across the world but my hope is that through it all, we will continue to support those in need, appreciate the reminder to value health, family and friends, and focus on the strength we find in our faith. This past year was certainly an incredible journey for my family and me, and we are so grateful that you were such a big part of it along the way. Regardless of the outcome of the election, one thing that will never change is the love we have for our state and our nation. We are so thankful for the culture, values and freedoms we hold dear and we will always continue to fight to protect them. Krysty, Ella, Ava and I are so thankful for the blessings in our life and the many friends we’ve gained – including you. We hope you have a wonderful Thanksgiving holiday. name: Wesley Hunt
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hunt for Congress
body:
Friend,

Emily and I want to wish you and your family a very happy Thanksgiving!

As we gather this year with family and friends, whether in person or in
spirit, Thanksgiving is the perfect time to reflect on the gifts God has
bestowed upon us.

I am thankful to have a loving and supportive wife and two healthy and
beautiful daughters. I am thankful to live in a city that provides incredible
opportunities to it's residents. But beyond anything else, I am thankful to be
an American and to share this great Nation with my fellow citizens. We are all
blessed to live as free men and women and to enjoy the liberty that our
soldiers fought to protect.

I hope you have a safe and happy Thanksgiving!

Sincerely,
Wesley

Paid for by Hunt for Congress. Military images and information do not imply
endorsement by DOD [Department of Defense] or any service branch.

name: Darin LaHood
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by LaHood for Congress
body:
>From my family to yours, I’m wishing you a blessed and very Happy Thanksgiving!

<>

Friend,

>From my family to yours, I want to wish you a blessed and very Happy
Thanksgiving!

For many Americans, this Thanksgiving will look different from any other. From
smaller, more intimate family celebrations to Zoom Thanksgiving calls with
relatives near and far, each of us will be celebrating in a way we haven’t done
before. Although our celebrations may change, what the day means doesn’t vary.

As difficult as it is to live in the midst of a global pandemic, Thanksgiving
remains a day to count all the blessings in our lives. And friend, there are
still so many reasons to be grateful.

Personally, I’m thankful for my family--for my wife and three sons--and for
the opportunity to serve you in Congress. I’m also thankful for our brave men
and women serving in the Armed Forces, who protect this great country we live
in. I’m thankful for our police and law enforcement officers, who put it on the
line to protect us and our communities. And in the time of COVID-19, I’m
especially thankful for all our front-line workers--for our doctors, nurses,
and first responders, who put themselves at risk to serve their neighbors in
need.

I wish you good health and a Happy Thanksgiving.

Darin LaHood

Paid for by LaHood for Congress

P.O. Box 10735 Peoria, IL 61612

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com

clean_body: From my family to yours, I’m wishing you a blessed and very Happy Thanksgiving! <> Friend, From my family to yours, I want to wish you a blessed and very Happy Thanksgiving! For many Americans, this Thanksgiving will look different from any other. From smaller, more intimate family celebrations to Zoom Thanksgiving calls with relatives near and far, each of us will be celebrating in a way we haven’t done before. Although our celebrations may change, what the day means doesn’t vary. As difficult as it is to live in the midst of a global pandemic, Thanksgiving remains a day to count all the blessings in our lives. And friend, there are still so many reasons to be grateful. Personally, I’m thankful for my family--for my wife and three sons--and for the opportunity to serve you in Congress. I’m also thankful for our brave men and women serving in the Armed Forces, who protect this great country we live in. I’m thankful for our police and law enforcement officers, who put it on the line to protect us and our communities. And in the time of COVID-19, I’m especially thankful for all our front-line workers--for our doctors, nurses, and first responders, who put themselves at risk to serve their neighbors in need. I wish you good health and a Happy Thanksgiving. Darin LaHood Paid for by LaHood for Congress P.O. Box 10735 Peoria, IL 61612

subject: Happy Thanksgiving from Milwaukee!
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/485d38b95d32/happy-thanksgiving-from-milwaukee?e=c2296cffff)
---
| |
---
| | Dear Fellow Patriot,

Greetings and happy thanksgiving to you and your family!

As we take this day to give thanks, despite the challenging year for all of us, let us be reminded of all the many blessings in our lives and especially for living in the greatest nation on earth.

Many of you know that my family and I immigrated to the U.S. from Iran when I was less than a year old in 1979. My parents struggled financially and socially, as we tried to learn a new language and assimilate in society, but one thing we never did was give up, and this country welcomed us with open arms and it never gave up on us!

Whether it was my late mother distributing Yellow Pages or starting her own business as a nanny, she persevered and no one told her she couldn’t do it because of her gender, or because she was Iranian. Or my father who studied to become a surgeon and built a successful practice through his hard work and determination to have his family live the American Dream, no government ever told him that he couldn’t be a physician.

All these are things I reflect on when I think of how different my life could have been if my parents had raised me in a country that didn’t value entrepreneurship and allowing people to control their own destiny rather than having the heavy hand of government dictating their professions and their livelihood.

As I write you, I am in Milwaukee, Wisconsin working as part of a volunteer legal team for the Trump Campaign. Lawyers and observers have been here since last Friday to ensure that every legal vote is counted, and every illegal vote is not counted.

We live in a country where our brave men and women fought and many died, for us to live in the greatest democracy on earth and to have free and fair elections, unlike my country of birth. For that reason, I am not only grateful to be a part of the team on the ground here to help with this recount effort, but I am thankful to live in a democracy that honors a system of transparency in our elections' process.

I’d like to leave you with my interview with former Ambassador Nancy Brinker, Founder of Susan G. Komen (in honor of her late sister). Ambassador Brinker's show, 'Conversations with Nancy’ airs weekly on Newsmax with my interview first airing last week. In it I discuss the recent elections, the Affordable Care Act, and what a blessing it was to run for Congress and hold positions on Capitol Hill and at the Pentagon that I could never have done in my own country of birth because I am a woman. Just one reason to be thankful not just today, but everyday for living in the greatest country on earth.

I wish you and your family a very happy thanksgiving! Please do send me your comments and thoughts as I always appreciate hearing from you!

All my best,
Amanda Makki

subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TROY DOWNING FOR MONTANA
body:
We have much to be thankful for |
---
| | [ ](https://troydowning.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7f12ce154379a6c3af4424424&id=0ab34753dd&e=a212bb87cc)
---
| |
# Gratitude
Even in uncertain times, we have much to be grateful for. We have love, family, friendship, health and opportunity for which we are truly grateful.

Heather and I would like to convey our gratitude to all of our family, friends and supporters out there. We hope this note finds you and yours well and that you are making the most of this day of thanks with reflection, gratitude, and joy.

Our thoughts and prayers go out to those struggling and to their families. We understand that there are many who cannot be with their loved ones right now, some who are sick, and/or isolated. Our hearts, thoughts, and prayers are with you and yours.

We are especially thankful today for our law enforcement officers and military personnel who are not with their families today, making sure we are safe.

We are also incredibly thankful for the front lines fighting the good fight and giving aid and comfort to those suffering during the Covid19 pandemic. We have seen, first hand, the struggles of our doctors, nurses, and healthcare professionals who are working long hours under trying circumstances. You have our appreciation, admiration, gratitude, support, and prayers.

May God Bless you and yours and fill your hearts with much to be grateful for.

-Troy and Heather Downing
---

subject: Happy Thanksgiving! 🇺🇸 🥧 🦃
body:
This year, more than any other I’m grateful to live in a state and country that prides itself on perseverance and overcoming adversity. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/willdouglasfortexas/thanksgiving-11-26-2020?e=4f010155df)
---
| | Friends,

Thanksgiving looks different for a lot of folks this year, my family’s included. This year, more than any other I’m grateful to live in a state and country that prides itself on perseverance and overcoming adversity.

I’m thankful for blue collar workers that went to work every single day to keep our society running. I’m thankful for small business owners and entrepreneurs who changed their business models overnight to work around government overreach to serve their communities and provide for their families.

Finally, I’m thankful for my family, friends, employees, and the thousands of Texans I met this year that make our state so great.

It’s because of them and that uniquely Texas spirit that makes me certain we will emerge stronger than ever before.

>From my family to yours, Happy Thanksgiving!

Thank you,
Will Douglas
---
